summary: DHH announces he's acquired a 10GB iPod, describing the effort to get it shipped internationally while avoiding import taxes. Without a Mac, he'll use the third-party XPlay software to manage music from a PC. The post is a brief, excited gadget acquisition update from the early iPod era.
key_points: DHH obtained a 10GB iPod after significant effort, rewrapping it to avoid a 25% import tax
The iPod's 2000-song capacity could hold nearly his entire MP3 collection
Without a Mac, he planned to use Mediafour's XPlay ($29.99) as a PC-based iTunes alternative
money_quotes: The 10 Gb iPod has finally, after much struggle and delay, decided to surrender and become mine.
Fitting 2000 tunes, it can almost hold my entire collection of mp3's.
Being short of a Macintosh, though, I'll be forced to go third party for the computer link-up and tune transfering.
key_insight: A lighthearted early-2000s gadget post capturing the excitement of iPod ownership and the workarounds PC users needed before Apple embraced Windows.
